{{Infobox artwork | image_file = Yashiro, Shimosuwa, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture 393-0000, Japan - panoramio.jpg | painting_alignment = right | image_size = 300px | title = Manji no Sekibutsu | caption = Front view | year = [[1660]] (Manji 3) | type = {{ill|Stone Buddha|ja|石仏}} | material = {{ill|Andesite|ja|安山岩}} (hornblende-pyroxene andesite) | height_metric = 2.6 | width_metric = 3.8 | metric_unit = m | accession = | city = {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} | museum = | website = {{URL|1=https://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000750/index.html|2=Manji no Sekibutsu (Shimosuwa Town)}} }}
'''Manji no Sekibutsu''' (万治の石仏) is a {{ill|stone Buddha statue|ja|石仏}} located in {{ill|Shimosuwa|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. It was created in the early Edo period in [[1660]] (Manji 3). Owned by Shimosuwa Town, it has been designated a Municipal Cultural Property since [[1982]] (Shōwa 57) under the name "Manji no Sekibutsu (Mitarashi no Sekibutsu)."<ref name=town>[http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000750/index.html Manji no Sekibutsu] - Shimosuwa Town, accessed February 11, 2021.</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1496969553133/files/5-106.pdf |title=Municipal Cultural Property No. 34 |access-date=2023/8/20 |publisher=Shimosuwa Town}}</ref> It is situated across the {{ill|Togawa River|ja|砥川}} from the {{ill|Suwa Taisha Shimosha Spring Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#下社}}. The local area name "Sekibutsu" is derived from this statue.
The stone Buddha gained national recognition in the [[1970s]] when it was introduced by the artist {{ill|Taro Okamoto|ja|岡本太郎}}.<ref name=kanko>[https://shimosuwaonsen.jp/万治の石仏/ Manji no Sekibutsu] - Shimosuwa Tourism Association, accessed February 11, 2021.</ref>
"Manji no Sekibutsu" is also a registered trademark owned by the Shimosuwa Chamber of Commerce.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://www.j-platpat.inpit.go.jp/c1800/TR/JP-1975-111530/F9955187CD2A8CF63F94F7E0BFD8C0A6B6A2FE9ED1871582677DE84CB61BA02A/40/ja |title=Trademark Registration No. 1326194 |access-date=2023/7/8 |publisher=National Center for Industrial Property Information and Training}}</ref>
== Overview == [[File:Manji-no sekibutsu (stone buddha) , 万治の石仏 - panoramio (20).jpg|thumb|Inscription "Namu Amida Butsu Manji 3rd Year, 11th Month, 1st Day, Ganshu Myōyo Jokō, Shinyo Hiroharu"]]
The stone Buddha measures 2.6 meters in height, 3.8 meters in width, and 3.7 meters in depth. It is made of andesite (hornblende-pyroxene andesite), with a Buddha head approximately 65 centimeters tall placed on a massive stone body.<ref name=kanko/><ref name=":4" /> The front of the body features a carved {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}} seated in {{ill|mudra|lt=Dhyanamudra|ja|印相#阿弥陀如来の印相}}.<ref name=town/><ref name=":4" />
The robe is adorned with symbols such as {{ill|Swastika|ja|卍}} (right-facing), the sun, thunder, clouds, rocks, and the moon, reflecting esoteric {{ill|Mikkyō|ja|密教}} mandalas. These elements are attributed to the "One Body, Two Buddhas" doctrine of a sect of sculpting priests from the {{ill|Jōdo-shū|ja|浄土宗}}, combining {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}} with {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name=town/><ref name=":4" />
== Legend == A local legend recounts that during the construction of a large {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} for Suwa Taisha in [[1657]] (Meireki 3), a stonecutter attempted to use the current stone Buddha as material. However, upon striking it with a chisel, the stone bled. Afraid of divine retribution, the construction ceased. The stone was later consecrated as an Amida Nyorai.<ref name=town/><ref name=kanko/>
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Manji-no-sekibutsu01n4592.jpg|Front view File:Manji-no-sekibutsu06n4592.jpg|Rear view File:Manji-no-sekibutsu05n4592.jpg|Face File:Manji-no-sekibutsu07s4592.jpg|Hand mudra </gallery>
== Access == * 16 minutes by bus from {{ill|Shimosuwa Station|ja|下諏訪駅}} (Miyanoue bus stop) * {{ill|National Route 20 (Japan)|ja|国道20号}}

{{Infobox artwork | image_file = Yashiro, Shimosuwa, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture 393-0000, Japan - panoramio.jpg | painting_alignment = right | image_size = 300px | title = Manji no Sekibutsu | caption = Front view | year = [[1660]] (Manji 3) | type = {{ill|Stone Buddha|ja|石仏}} | material = {{ill|Andesite|ja|安山岩}} (hornblende-pyroxene andesite) | height_metric = 2.6 | width_metric = 3.8 | metric_unit = m | accession = | city = {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} | museum = | website = {{URL|1=https://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000750/index.html|2=Manji no Sekibutsu (Shimosuwa Town)}} }}
'''Manji no Sekibutsu''' (万治の石仏) is a {{ill|stone Buddha statue|ja|石仏}} located in {{ill|Shimosuwa|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. It was created in the early Edo period in [[1660]] (Manji 3). Owned by Shimosuwa Town, it has been designated a Municipal Cultural Property since [[1982]] (Shōwa 57) under the name "Manji no Sekibutsu (Mitarashi no Sekibutsu)."<ref name=town>[http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000750/index.html Manji no Sekibutsu] - Shimosuwa Town, accessed February 11, 2021.</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1496969553133/files/5-106.pdf |title=Municipal Cultural Property No. 34 |access-date=2023/8/20 |publisher=Shimosuwa Town}}</ref> It is situated across the {{ill|Togawa River|ja|砥川}} from the {{ill|Suwa Taisha Shimosha Spring Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#下社}}. The local area name "Sekibutsu" is derived from this statue.
The stone Buddha gained national recognition in the [[1970s]] when it was introduced by the artist {{ill|Taro Okamoto|ja|岡本太郎}}.<ref name=kanko>[https://shimosuwaonsen.jp/万治の石仏/ Manji no Sekibutsu] - Shimosuwa Tourism Association, accessed February 11, 2021.</ref>
"Manji no Sekibutsu" is also a registered trademark owned by the Shimosuwa Chamber of Commerce.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://www.j-platpat.inpit.go.jp/c1800/TR/JP-1975-111530/F9955187CD2A8CF63F94F7E0BFD8C0A6B6A2FE9ED1871582677DE84CB61BA02A/40/ja |title=Trademark Registration No. 1326194 |access-date=2023/7/8 |publisher=National Center for Industrial Property Information and Training}}</ref>
== Overview == [[File:Manji-no sekibutsu (stone buddha) , 万治の石仏 - panoramio (20).jpg|thumb|Inscription "Namu Amida Butsu Manji 3rd Year, 11th Month, 1st Day, Ganshu Myōyo Jokō, Shinyo Hiroharu"]]
The stone Buddha measures 2.6 meters in height, 3.8 meters in width, and 3.7 meters in depth. It is made of andesite (hornblende-pyroxene andesite), with a Buddha head approximately 65 centimeters tall placed on a massive stone body.<ref name=kanko/><ref name=":4" /> The front of the body features a carved {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}} seated in {{ill|mudra|lt=Dhyanamudra|ja|印相#阿弥陀如来の印相}}.<ref name=town/><ref name=":4" />
The robe is adorned with symbols such as {{ill|Swastika|ja|卍}} (right-facing), the sun, thunder, clouds, rocks, and the moon, reflecting esoteric {{ill|Mikkyō|ja|密教}} mandalas. These elements are attributed to the "One Body, Two Buddhas" doctrine of a sect of sculpting priests from the {{ill|Jōdo-shū|ja|浄土宗}}, combining {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}} with {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name=town/><ref name=":4" />
== Legend == A local legend recounts that during the construction of a large {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} for Suwa Taisha in [[1657]] (Meireki 3), a stonecutter attempted to use the current stone Buddha as material. However, upon striking it with a chisel, the stone bled. Afraid of divine retribution, the construction ceased. The stone was later consecrated as an Amida Nyorai.<ref name=town/><ref name=kanko/>
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Manji-no-sekibutsu01n4592.jpg|Front view File:Manji-no-sekibutsu06n4592.jpg|Rear view File:Manji-no-sekibutsu05n4592.jpg|Face File:Manji-no-sekibutsu07s4592.jpg|Hand mudra </gallery>
== Access == * 16 minutes by bus from {{ill|Shimosuwa Station|ja|下諏訪駅}} (Miyanoue bus stop) * {{ill|National Route 20 (Japan)|ja|国道20号}}
== Nearby == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha Shimosha Spring Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#下社}} * {{ill|Jiunji Temple (Nagano)|ja|慈雲寺 (長野県下諏訪町)}} * {{ill|Suigetsu Park|ja|水月公園}} * Grave of {{ill|Yamatokentomotsu|ja|大和監物}} * {{ill|Yajoseki|ja|矢除石}}
== References == {{Reflist|25em}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Landmarks of Shimosuwa|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事}} * {{ill|Taro Okamoto|ja|岡本太郎}} * {{ill|Jiro Nitta|ja|新田次郎}} * {{ill|Kazuo Ichikawa|ja|市川一雄}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox artwork | image_file = Yashiro, Shimosuwa, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture 393-0000, Japan - panoramio.jpg | painting_alignment = right | image_size = 300px | title = 万治の石仏 | caption = 正面から | year = [[1660年]]（[[万治]]3年） | type = [[石仏]] | material = [[安山岩]]（含[[角閃石]]普通[[輝石]]安山岩） | height_metric = 2.6 | width_metric = 3.8 | metric_unit = m | accession = | city = [[長野県]][[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]東山田字石仏 | museum = | website = {{URL|1=https://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000750/index.html|2=万治の石仏（下諏訪町）}} |other_title_1=萬治の石佛}}
'''万治の石仏'''（まんじのせきぶつ）は、[[長野県]][[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]東山田字石仏にある、[[江戸時代]]前期の[[1660年]]（[[万治]]3年）に造られた[[石仏]]。所有者は下諏訪町で、同町の指定有形[[文化財]]に指定されている（[[1982年]]（[[昭和]]57年）[[3月26日]]指定、登録名「万治の石仏（みたらしの石仏）」）<ref name=town>[http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000750/index.html 万治の石仏] - 下諏訪町、2021年2月11日閲覧。</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1496969553133/files/5-106.pdf |title=町指定文化財 No.34 |access-date=2023/8/20 |publisher=下諏訪町}}</ref>。[[砥川 (長野県)|砥川]]を挟み、[[諏訪大社#下社|諏訪大社下社春宮]]の対岸に位置する。所在地の小地名「石仏」も同石仏に由来する。
[[1970年代]]に[[芸術家]]の[[岡本太郎]]が紹介したことで日本全国に広く知られるようになった<ref name=kanko>[https://shimosuwaonsen.jp/万治の石仏/ 万治の石仏] - 下諏訪観光協会、2021年2月11日閲覧。</ref>。
「万治の石仏」は、下諏訪[[商工会議所]]を権利者とする[[登録商標]]でもある<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.j-platpat.inpit.go.jp/c1800/TR/JP-1975-111530/F9955187CD2A8CF63F94F7E0BFD8C0A6B6A2FE9ED1871582677DE84CB61BA02A/40/ja |title=商標出願･登録情報 登録1326194 |access-date=2023/7/8 |publisher=独立行政法人工業所有権情報・研修館}}</ref>。
== 概要 == [[File:Manji-no sekibutsu (stone buddha) , 万治の石仏 - panoramio (20).jpg|thumb|「万治の石仏」の名の由来である胴体脇の銘「[[南無阿弥陀仏]] 万治三年[[11月1日 (旧暦)|十一月一日]] 願主 明誉浄光 心誉廣春」<ref group=注釈>ただし、願主の明誉浄光と心誉廣春の2人は僧籍に見当たらない。</ref>]] 高さ2.6メートル、幅3.8メートル、奥行き3.7メートルの[[安山岩]]（含[[角閃石]]普通[[輝石]]安山岩）をそのまま胴体とし、その上に高さ約65センチメートルの仏頭を乗せた石仏である<ref name=kanko/><ref name=":4" />。胴体正面には[[印相#阿弥陀如来の印相|定印]]を結んだ[[阿弥陀如来]]の坐像が彫られる<ref name=town/><ref name=":4" />。衣の上には向かって右から[[卍|右卍]]、[[太陽]]・[[雷]]・[[雲]]・[[磐座]]・[[月]]など[[密教]]の[[曼荼羅]]が刻まれ、これらは[[木食]][[上人]]・弾誓(たんぜい)に始まる[[浄土宗]]の作仏[[聖]](さぶつひじり)一派による「同体異仏」（一体の仏像に阿弥陀如来と[[大日如来]]を共存させる）の表現とされる<ref name=town/><ref name=":4" />。願主の明誉浄光および心誉廣春は、それぞれ清念(法名・明誉)、説難(法名・心誉)として作仏聖の系図に名を連ねており<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=謎の石仏 作仏聖の足跡 |date=1993年4月30日 |publisher=角川書店 |pages=148-150,152 |author=宮島潤子 |series=角川選書238 |isbn=4-04-703238-7}}</ref>、明誉が同石仏の仏頭を刻み、心誉が胴体の彫刻を受け持ったと考えられている<ref name=":4" />。
江戸時代の史料には「えぼし石」{{Refnest|group=注釈|[[1673年]]（[[延宝]]3年）の[[検地帳]]や[[1773年]]（[[享保]]18年）の『諏訪藩主手元絵図』による<ref name=town/>。}}や「みたらしの石仏」{{Refnest|group=注釈|[[1805年]]（[[文化 (元号)|文化]]2年）の下の原村『山の神講歳代記』による<ref name=town/>。}}の名称で記録されており<ref name=town/>、地元の人々からは「あみだ様」と呼ばれてきた<ref name=town/><ref name=":4">{{Cite book|和書 |title=謎の石仏 作仏聖の足跡 |date=1993-04-30 |publisher=角川書店 |pages=10-20,219-222 |series=角川選書238 |isbn=4-04-703238-7 |author=宮島潤子}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=諏訪の石仏 |date=1971/2/15 |publisher=諏訪教育会 |pages=71-72 |author=今井廣亀}}</ref>。
胴体正面向かって左側に「万治三年」の銘が刻まれていることにちなみ、近年になって「万治の石仏」の名称が生まれている。公知の文献としては、[[1932年]]（[[昭和]]7年）の刊行物における有賀恭一による「諏訪の石」の解説中に、「萬治の石佛」の記述が見られる<ref>{{Cite journal|和書|author=有賀恭一|year=1932|date=1932-07-25|title=諏訪の石|journal=郷土|volume=2|issue=1-3|pages=178-202|publisher=郷土発行所}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=石（昭和7年刊『郷土』復刻版） |publisher=木耳社 |pages=178-202 |editor=池上隆祐 |date=1978-06-05}}</ref><ref name=":3">{{Cite book|和書 |title=謎の石仏 作仏聖の足跡 |date=1993-04-30 |publisher=角川書店 |page=232 |author=宮島潤子 |series=角川選書238 |isbn=4-04-703238-7}}</ref>。また地元の考古学者・中村龍雄は、[[1961年]]（[[昭和]]36年）5月28日の下諏訪町博物館（現・[[諏訪湖博物館]]）主催の町内史跡巡りの際の図面や案内順列表に「万治の石仏（阿弥陀如来）」の記載があると述べている<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=万治石佛の謎 |year=1975 |publisher=自主出版 |pages=10,28 |author=中村龍口（中村龍雄）}}</ref>。
この石仏が全国的に知られるようになったきっかけは、[[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）に中村龍雄、地域紙「[[湖国新聞]]」編集長・[[市川一雄]]らが、[[諏訪大社]]の視察に訪れた岡本太郎を現地に案内したことにある<ref>{{Cite journal|和書|date=1974-5-28|title=おんばしらに強い興味 前衛美術家岡本太郎さんが調査に 「本来の姿」「スワ族」に独自の見解のべる|journal=湖国新聞|issue=9116|page=4|publisher=湖国新聞社|location=長野県諏訪郡下諏訪町}}</ref><ref>{{Cite journal|和書|date=1974-8-13|title=新名所、万治の石仏 岡本さんが”発見”、紹介|journal=湖国新聞|issue=9192|page=4|publisher=湖国新聞社|location=長野県諏訪郡下諏訪町}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite book|和書 |title=と川石人語り |year=2002 |publisher=草風社 |pages=97-112 |month=11 |author=市川一雄}}</ref>。そこで同石仏を鑑賞した岡本が絶賛し、全国紙のコラムなどに掲載した<ref>{{Cite journal|和書|author=岡本太郎|date=1975-06-24|title=諏訪の石仏|journal=朝日新聞東京夕刊|publisher=朝日新聞社}}</ref><ref>{{Cite journal|和書|author=岡本太郎|month=6|year=1980|title=諏訪「御柱祭」|journal=芸術新潮|volume=31|issue=7|page=147|publisher=新潮社}}</ref><ref name=":1" />。さらに、[[上諏訪町]]（現・[[諏訪市]]）出身の小説家・[[新田次郎]]もこれに着目し<ref name="kanko" />、同石仏は[[イースター島]]の石人の頭部が日本へもたらされたものとする大胆な想定を基にして小説『万治の石仏』を著している<ref>{{Cite journal|和書|author=新田次郎|month=9|year=1976|title=万治の石仏|journal=オール讀物|volume=31|issue=9|pages=32-59|publisher=文芸春秋}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=鷲ヶ峰物語 |publisher=講談社 |author=新田次郎 |date=1977/2/24 |pages=121-173}}</ref><ref name=":1" />。これらにより、「万治の石仏」の名称が定着し、知名度も上昇<ref name=":3" />して全国的な観光名所となっていく。
これ以降、「万治の石仏」の名称を[[産業財産権]]として利用する動きも活性化する。市川一雄と交友関係にあり、下諏訪町で製菓業と学習塾を営む傍ら、当時湖国新聞でコラムを連載していた矢ヶ崎孫次によって、「万治の石仏」を菓子類の商品名として使用するための[[商標登録]]出願（[[1975年]]9月6日、区分：菓子、パン）がなされている<ref name=":0" /><ref name=":2">{{Cite journal|和書|author=矢ヶ崎孫次|date=1977-10-13|title=秋の夜長に狂った|journal=湖国新聞|issue=10316|page=3|publisher=湖国新聞社|location=長野県諏訪郡下諏訪町}}</ref>{{Refnest|group=注釈|[[2008年]]（[[平成]]20年）に矢ヶ崎から下諏訪商工会議所へ権利譲渡された<ref name=nippo/>。}}。続いて、別の出願人から他区分（調味料、穀物の加工品、べんとう）での出願も行われている<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.j-platpat.inpit.go.jp/c1800/TR/JP-1996-106602/4286104892F72437F8981620903E6A9E1B62D47C13B9AD10DE9481FA32939FB4/40/ja |title=商標出願･登録情報 登録4173914 |access-date=2023/7/8 |publisher=独立行政法人工業所有権情報・研修館}}</ref>。
なお、市川一雄と矢ヶ崎孫次はそれぞれ、自身が「万治の石仏」[[命名]]者であると述懐している<ref name=":1" /><ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=諏訪式。 |date=2020-10-02 |publisher=亜紀書房 |pages=217-219 |author=小倉美惠子 |isbn=978-4-7505-1665-3}}</ref><ref name=":2" /><ref name="nippo">[http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/articles/68655 「万治の石仏」名付け親 30年ぶり下諏訪に] - 『[[長野日報]]』2020年10月16日、2021年2月11日閲覧。</ref>{{Refnest|group=注釈|市川は、[[2002年]]（[[平成]]14年）および[[2020年]]（[[令和]]2年）に「万治の石仏」の名は湖国新聞の記事(1974年8月13日)が初出である旨を述べている。 矢ヶ崎は、[[1977]]年（[[昭和]]52年）に「市川梶郎（市川一雄のペンネーム）は「万治の石仏」の事実上の名づけ親であり、その名称を早速に商標登録出願したオレは策略的アキンドだ」と述べる一方、[[2020年]]（[[令和]]2年）になって「何の工夫もなく付けた名前だが、今になってみれば良い名前だったと思う」、「これからも多くの人に愛される石仏であってほしい」と回想している。}}。実際には既存の名称であったことは先述の通りであり、両者いずれも命名者には当たらないが、湖国新聞などのメディアを通じた1970年代中盤の両者の活動も、同石仏の知名度の向上に貢献したと言える。
[[2019年]]（[[令和]]元年）以降の[[新型コロナウイルス感染症 (2019年)|新型コロナウイルス感染症]]拡大下にある近年は「万治」が「よろずおさまる」に通じることから注目が高まっており<ref name=hokuriku>[https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000024497 「万ず治まる」人々願い 下諏訪の万治の石仏グッズ人気（信濃毎日新聞（2020年7月7日））] - 北陸新幹線で行こう!北陸・信越観光ナビ、2021年2月11日閲覧。</ref><ref name=nippo/>、石仏がモチーフの関連グッズの人気が高まっているという<ref name=hokuriku/>。
== 伝承 == 「万治の石仏」には、造立にまつわる以下の伝承がある。
[[1657年]]（[[明暦]]3年）に第3代[[諏訪藩|諏訪高島藩]]主の[[諏訪忠晴]]から諏訪大社下社春宮に石の大[[鳥居]]を立てるよう命じられた石工が、現在石仏となっている大石を材料にしようと[[鑿]]を入れたところ、そこから[[血液|血]]が出てきたため祟りを恐れて作業を中止した<ref name=town/><ref name=kanko/>。その晩に夢枕で上原山（現在の[[茅野市]]）に良い石材があると告げられた石工が探しに行くとその通りであり、同山の石で鳥居を造る代わりに大石を阿弥陀如来として祀ったという<ref name=town/><ref name=kanko/>。石仏に残る鑿の跡はその時のものとされる<ref name=town/>。
== 伸びる首 == [[1991年]]（平成3年）[[8月]]に一度頭部が落下したため支柱で固定し修復したが、その後、ある[[写真家]]が毎年撮った[[写真]]を見たところ、首が伸びていることがわかった。[[2007年]]（平成19年）に[[テレビ番組]]で紹介され、観光客も増えたが、下諏訪観光協会は「周辺の安全確保」ためとして再修復することとし、[[2008年]]（平成20年）[[3月4日]]に修復作業が行われ、頭部の下から[[水]]や[[泥]]、[[賽銭]]25円が発見された。首が伸びた原因は、以前の修復時に設けた支柱に水が溜まり、[[氷]]になって斜めになり、その繰り返しで頭部が上昇したとされる。修復前に測定したところ、正面で4cm、左右は6～7cm上昇していた。
== ギャラリー == <gallery> 画像:Manji-no-sekibutsu01n4592.jpg|前方 画像:Manji-no-sekibutsu06n4592.jpg|後方 画像:Manji-no-sekibutsu05n4592.jpg|顔 画像:Manji-no-sekibutsu07s4592.jpg|印 </gallery>
== アクセス == * [[下諏訪駅]]より[[バス (交通機関)|バス]]で16分（宮の上バス停） * [[国道20号]]
== 周辺 == * [[諏訪大社#春宮|諏訪大社下社春宮]] * [[慈雲寺 (長野県下諏訪町)|慈雲寺]] * [[水月公園]] * [[大和監物]]の墓 * [[矢除石]]
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group=注釈}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|25em}}
== 関連項目 == *[[下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事|下諏町の名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事]] *[[岡本太郎]] *[[新田次郎]] *[[市川一雄]]
== 外部リンク == * [https://shimosuwaonsen.jp/万治の石仏/ 万治の石仏] - 下諏訪観光協会 * [https://www.j-platpat.inpit.go.jp/c1800/TR/JP-1975-111530/F9955187CD2A8CF63F94F7E0BFD8C0A6B6A2FE9ED1871582677DE84CB61BA02A/40/ja 登録1326194 万治の石仏] - 特許情報プラットフォーム * {{Commonscat-inline}}

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[万治の石仏]] to [[Manji Stone Buddha]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{Infobox artwork | image_file = Yashiro, Shimosuwa, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture 393-0000, Japan - panoramio.jpg | painting_alignment = right | image_size = 300px | title = 万治の石仏 | caption = 正面から | year = [[1660年]]（[[万治]]3年） | type = [[石仏]] | material = [[安山岩]]（含[[角閃石]]普通[[輝石]]安山岩） | height_metric = 2.6 | width_metric = 3.8 | metric_unit = m | accession = | city = [[長野県]][[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]東山田字石仏 | museum = | website = {{URL|1=https://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000750/index.html|2=万治の石仏（下諏訪町）}} |other_title_1=萬治の石佛}}
'''万治の石仏'''（まんじのせきぶつ）は、[[長野県]][[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]東山田字石仏にある、[[江戸時代]]前期の[[1660年]]（[[万治]]3年）に造られた[[石仏]]。所有者は下諏訪町で、同町の指定有形[[文化財]]に指定されている（[[1982年]]（[[昭和]]57年）[[3月26日]]指定、登録名「万治の石仏（みたらしの石仏）」）<ref name=town>[http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000750/index.html 万治の石仏] - 下諏訪町、2021年2月11日閲覧。</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1496969553133/files/5-106.pdf |title=町指定文化財 No.34 |access-date=2023/8/20 |publisher=下諏訪町}}</ref>。[[砥川 (長野県)|砥川]]を挟み、[[諏訪大社#下社|諏訪大社下社春宮]]の対岸に位置する。所在地の小地名「石仏」も同石仏に由来する。
[[1970年代]]に[[芸術家]]の[[岡本太郎]]が紹介したことで日本全国に広く知られるようになった<ref name=kanko>[https://shimosuwaonsen.jp/万治の石仏/ 万治の石仏] - 下諏訪観光協会、2021年2月11日閲覧。</ref>。
「万治の石仏」は、下諏訪[[商工会議所]]を権利者とする[[登録商標]]でもある<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.j-platpat.inpit.go.jp/c1800/TR/JP-1975-111530/F9955187CD2A8CF63F94F7E0BFD8C0A6B6A2FE9ED1871582677DE84CB61BA02A/40/ja |title=商標出願･登録情報 登録1326194 |access-date=2023/7/8 |publisher=独立行政法人工業所有権情報・研修館}}</ref>。
== 概要 == [[File:Manji-no sekibutsu (stone buddha) , 万治の石仏 - panoramio (20).jpg|thumb|「万治の石仏」の名の由来である胴体脇の銘「[[南無阿弥陀仏]] 万治三年[[11月1日 (旧暦)|十一月一日]] 願主 明誉浄光 心誉廣春」<ref group=注釈>ただし、願主の明誉浄光と心誉廣春の2人は僧籍に見当たらない。</ref>]] 高さ2.6メートル、幅3.8メートル、奥行き3.7メートルの[[安山岩]]（含[[角閃石]]普通[[輝石]]安山岩）をそのまま胴体とし、その上に高さ約65センチメートルの仏頭を乗せた石仏である<ref name=kanko/><ref name=":4" />。胴体正面には[[印相#阿弥陀如来の印相|定印]]を結んだ[[阿弥陀如来]]の坐像が彫られる<ref name=town/><ref name=":4" />。衣の上には向かって右から[[卍|右卍]]、[[太陽]]・[[雷]]・[[雲]]・[[磐座]]・[[月]]など[[密教]]の[[曼荼羅]]が刻まれ、これらは[[木食]][[上人]]・弾誓(たんぜい)に始まる[[浄土宗]]の作仏[[聖]](さぶつひじり)一派による「同体異仏」（一体の仏像に阿弥陀如来と[[大日如来]]を共存させる）の表現とされる<ref name=town/><ref name=":4" />。願主の明誉浄光および心誉廣春は、それぞれ清念(法名・明誉)、説難(法名・心誉)として作仏聖の系図に名を連ねており<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=謎の石仏 作仏聖の足跡 |date=1993年4月30日 |publisher=角川書店 |pages=148-150,152 |author=宮島潤子 |series=角川選書238 |isbn=4-04-703238-7}}</ref>、明誉が同石仏の仏頭を刻み、心誉が胴体の彫刻を受け持ったと考えられている<ref name=":4" />。
江戸時代の史料には「えぼし石」{{Refnest|group=注釈|[[1673年]]（[[延宝]]3年）の[[検地帳]]や[[1773年]]（[[享保]]18年）の『諏訪藩主手元絵図』による<ref name=town/>。}}や「みたらしの石仏」{{Refnest|group=注釈|[[1805年]]（[[文化 (元号)|文化]]2年）の下の原村『山の神講歳代記』による<ref name=town/>。}}の名称で記録されており<ref name=town/>、地元の人々からは「あみだ様」と呼ばれてきた<ref name=town/><ref name=":4">{{Cite book|和書 |title=謎の石仏 作仏聖の足跡 |date=1993-04-30 |publisher=角川書店 |pages=10-20,219-222 |series=角川選書238 |isbn=4-04-703238-7 |author=宮島潤子}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=諏訪の石仏 |date=1971/2/15 |publisher=諏訪教育会 |pages=71-72 |author=今井廣亀}}</ref>。
胴体正面向かって左側に「万治三年」の銘が刻まれていることにちなみ、近年になって「万治の石仏」の名称が生まれている。公知の文献としては、[[1932年]]（[[昭和]]7年）の刊行物における有賀恭一による「諏訪の石」の解説中に、「萬治の石佛」の記述が見られる<ref>{{Cite journal|和書|author=有賀恭一|year=1932|date=1932-07-25|title=諏訪の石|journal=郷土|volume=2|issue=1-3|pages=178-202|publisher=郷土発行所}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=石（昭和7年刊『郷土』復刻版） |publisher=木耳社 |pages=178-202 |editor=池上隆祐 |date=1978-06-05}}</ref><ref name=":3">{{Cite book|和書 |title=謎の石仏 作仏聖の足跡 |date=1993-04-30 |publisher=角川書店 |page=232 |author=宮島潤子 |series=角川選書238 |isbn=4-04-703238-7}}</ref>。また地元の考古学者・中村龍雄は、[[1961年]]（[[昭和]]36年）5月28日の下諏訪町博物館（現・[[諏訪湖博物館]]）主催の町内史跡巡りの際の図面や案内順列表に「万治の石仏（阿弥陀如来）」の記載があると述べている<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=万治石佛の謎 |year=1975 |publisher=自主出版 |pages=10,28 |author=中村龍口（中村龍雄）}}</ref>。
この石仏が全国的に知られるようになったきっかけは、[[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）に中村龍雄、地域紙「[[湖国新聞]]」編集長・[[市川一雄]]らが、[[諏訪大社]]の視察に訪れた岡本太郎を現地に案内したことにある<ref>{{Cite journal|和書|date=1974-5-28|title=おんばしらに強い興味 前衛美術家岡本太郎さんが調査に 「本来の姿」「スワ族」に独自の見解のべる|journal=湖国新聞|issue=9116|page=4|publisher=湖国新聞社|location=長野県諏訪郡下諏訪町}}</ref><ref>{{Cite journal|和書|date=1974-8-13|title=新名所、万治の石仏 岡本さんが”発見”、紹介|journal=湖国新聞|issue=9192|page=4|publisher=湖国新聞社|location=長野県諏訪郡下諏訪町}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite book|和書 |title=と川石人語り |year=2002 |publisher=草風社 |pages=97-112 |month=11 |author=市川一雄}}</ref>。そこで同石仏を鑑賞した岡本が絶賛し、全国紙のコラムなどに掲載した<ref>{{Cite journal|和書|author=岡本太郎|date=1975-06-24|title=諏訪の石仏|journal=朝日新聞東京夕刊|publisher=朝日新聞社}}</ref><ref>{{Cite journal|和書|author=岡本太郎|month=6|year=1980|title=諏訪「御柱祭」|journal=芸術新潮|volume=31|issue=7|page=147|publisher=新潮社}}</ref><ref name=":1" />。さらに、[[上諏訪町]]（現・[[諏訪市]]）出身の小説家・[[新田次郎]]もこれに着目し<ref name="kanko" />、同石仏は[[イースター島]]の石人の頭部が日本へもたらされたものとする大胆な想定を基にして小説『万治の石仏』を著している<ref>{{Cite journal|和書|author=新田次郎|month=9|year=1976|title=万治の石仏|journal=オール讀物|volume=31|issue=9|pages=32-59|publisher=文芸春秋}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=鷲ヶ峰物語 |publisher=講談社 |author=新田次郎 |date=1977/2/24 |pages=121-173}}</ref><ref name=":1" />。これらにより、「万治の石仏」の名称が定着し、知名度も上昇<ref name=":3" />して全国的な観光名所となっていく。
これ以降、「万治の石仏」の名称を[[産業財産権]]として利用する動きも活性化する。市川一雄と交友関係にあり、下諏訪町で製菓業と学習塾を営む傍ら、当時湖国新聞でコラムを連載していた矢ヶ崎孫次によって、「万治の石仏」を菓子類の商品名として使用するための[[商標登録]]出願（[[1975年]]9月6日、区分：菓子、パン）がなされている<ref name=":0" /><ref name=":2">{{Cite journal|和書|author=矢ヶ崎孫次|date=1977-10-13|title=秋の夜長に狂った|journal=湖国新聞|issue=10316|page=3|publisher=湖国新聞社|location=長野県諏訪郡下諏訪町}}</ref>{{Refnest|group=注釈|[[2008年]]（[[平成]]20年）に矢ヶ崎から下諏訪商工会議所へ権利譲渡された<ref name=nippo/>。}}。続いて、別の出願人から他区分（調味料、穀物の加工品、べんとう）での出願も行われている<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.j-platpat.inpit.go.jp/c1800/TR/JP-1996-106602/4286104892F72437F8981620903E6A9E1B62D47C13B9AD10DE9481FA32939FB4/40/ja |title=商標出願･登録情報 登録4173914 |access-date=2023/7/8 |publisher=独立行政法人工業所有権情報・研修館}}</ref>。
なお、市川一雄と矢ヶ崎孫次はそれぞれ、自身が「万治の石仏」[[命名]]者であると述懐している<ref name=":1" /><ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=諏訪式。 |date=2020-10-02 |publisher=亜紀書房 |pages=217-219 |author=小倉美惠子 |isbn=978-4-7505-1665-3}}</ref><ref name=":2" /><ref name="nippo">[http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/articles/68655 「万治の石仏」名付け親 30年ぶり下諏訪に] - 『[[長野日報]]』2020年10月16日、2021年2月11日閲覧。</ref>{{Refnest|group=注釈|市川は、[[2002年]]（[[平成]]14年）および[[2020年]]（[[令和]]2年）に「万治の石仏」の名は湖国新聞の記事(1974年8月13日)が初出である旨を述べている。 矢ヶ崎は、[[1977]]年（[[昭和]]52年）に「市川梶郎（市川一雄のペンネーム）は「万治の石仏」の事実上の名づけ親であり、その名称を早速に商標登録出願したオレは策略的アキンドだ」と述べる一方、[[2020年]]（[[令和]]2年）になって「何の工夫もなく付けた名前だが、今になってみれば良い名前だったと思う」、「これからも多くの人に愛される石仏であってほしい」と回想している。}}。実際には既存の名称であったことは先述の通りであり、両者いずれも命名者には当たらないが、湖国新聞などのメディアを通じた1970年代中盤の両者の活動も、同石仏の知名度の向上に貢献したと言える。
[[2019年]]（[[令和]]元年）以降の[[新型コロナウイルス感染症 (2019年)|新型コロナウイルス感染症]]拡大下にある近年は「万治」が「よろずおさまる」に通じることから注目が高まっており<ref name=hokuriku>[https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000024497 「万ず治まる」人々願い 下諏訪の万治の石仏グッズ人気（信濃毎日新聞（2020年7月7日））] - 北陸新幹線で行こう!北陸・信越観光ナビ、2021年2月11日閲覧。</ref><ref name=nippo/>、石仏がモチーフの関連グッズの人気が高まっているという<ref name=hokuriku/>。
== 伝承 == 「万治の石仏」には、造立にまつわる以下の伝承がある。
[[1657年]]（[[明暦]]3年）に第3代[[諏訪藩|諏訪高島藩]]主の[[諏訪忠晴]]から諏訪大社下社春宮に石の大[[鳥居]]を立てるよう命じられた石工が、現在石仏となっている大石を材料にしようと[[鑿]]を入れたところ、そこから[[血液|血]]が出てきたため祟りを恐れて作業を中止した<ref name=town/><ref name=kanko/>。その晩に夢枕で上原山（現在の[[茅野市]]）に良い石材があると告げられた石工が探しに行くとその通りであり、同山の石で鳥居を造る代わりに大石を阿弥陀如来として祀ったという<ref name=town/><ref name=kanko/>。石仏に残る鑿の跡はその時のものとされる<ref name=town/>。
== 伸びる首 == [[1991年]]（平成3年）[[8月]]に一度頭部が落下したため支柱で固定し修復したが、その後、ある[[写真家]]が毎年撮った[[写真]]を見たところ、首が伸びていることがわかった。[[2007年]]（平成19年）に[[テレビ番組]]で紹介され、観光客も増えたが、下諏訪観光協会は「周辺の安全確保」ためとして再修復することとし、[[2008年]]（平成20年）[[3月4日]]に修復作業が行われ、頭部の下から[[水]]や[[泥]]、[[賽銭]]25円が発見された。首が伸びた原因は、以前の修復時に設けた支柱に水が溜まり、[[氷]]になって斜めになり、その繰り返しで頭部が上昇したとされる。修復前に測定したところ、正面で4cm、左右は6～7cm上昇していた。
== ギャラリー == <gallery> 画像:Manji-no-sekibutsu01n4592.jpg|前方 画像:Manji-no-sekibutsu06n4592.jpg|後方 画像:Manji-no-sekibutsu05n4592.jpg|顔 画像:Manji-no-sekibutsu07s4592.jpg|印 </gallery>
== アクセス == * [[下諏訪駅]]より[[バス (交通機関)|バス]]で16分（宮の上バス停） * [[国道20号]]
== 周辺 == * [[諏訪大社#春宮|諏訪大社下社春宮]] * [[慈雲寺 (長野県下諏訪町)|慈雲寺]] * [[水月公園]] * [[大和監物]]の墓 * [[矢除石]]
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group=注釈}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|25em}}
== 関連項目 == *[[下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事|下諏町の名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事]] *[[岡本太郎]] *[[新田次郎]] *[[市川一雄]]
== 外部リンク == * [https://shimosuwaonsen.jp/万治の石仏/ 万治の石仏] - 下諏訪観光協会 * [https://www.j-platpat.inpit.go.jp/c1800/TR/JP-1975-111530/F9955187CD2A8CF63F94F7E0BFD8C0A6B6A2FE9ED1871582677DE84CB61BA02A/40/ja 登録1326194 万治の石仏] - 特許情報プラットフォーム * {{Commonscat-inline}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Manji-no-sekibutsu]][[ja:万治の石仏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11353878]] Wikidata name: Manji no Sekibutsu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox artwork | image_file = Yashiro, Shimosuwa, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture 393-0000, Japan - panoramio.jpg | painting_alignment = right | image_size = 300px | title = Manji no Sekibutsu | caption = Front view | year = [[:en:1660|1660]] (Manji 3) | type = {{ill|Stone Buddha|ja|石仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Buddha|qq=}} | material = [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] (hornblende-pyroxene andesite) | height_metric = 2.6 | width_metric = 3.8 | metric_unit = m | accession = | city = [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] | museum = | website = {{URL|1=https://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000750/index.html|2=Manji no Sekibutsu (Shimosuwa Town)}} }}
'''Manji no Sekibutsu''' (万治の石仏) is a {{ill|Stone Buddha|lt=Stone Buddha|lt=Stone Buddha statue|ja|石仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone Buddha statue|qq=}} located in [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. It was created in the early Edo period in [[:en:1660|1660]] (Manji 3). Owned by Shimosuwa Town, it has been designated a Municipal Cultural Property since [[:en:1982|1982]] (Shōwa 57) under the name "Manji no Sekibutsu (Mitarashi no Sekibutsu)."<ref name=town>[http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000750/index.html Manji no Sekibutsu] - Shimosuwa Town, accessed February 11, 2021.</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1496969553133/files/5-106.pdf |title=Municipal Cultural Property No. 34 |access-date=2023/8/20 |publisher=Shimosuwa Town}}</ref> It is situated across the {{ill|Togawa River|ja|砥川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togawa River|qq=}} from the {{ill|Suwa Taisha Shimosha Spring Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#下社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha Shimosha Spring Shrine|qq=|1=Suwa taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q218813}}. The local area name "Sekibutsu" is derived from this statue.
The stone Buddha gained national recognition in the [[:en:1970s|1970s]] when it was introduced by the artist [[:en:Tarō Okamoto|Taro Okamoto]].<ref name=kanko>[https://shimosuwaonsen.jp/万治の石仏/ Manji no Sekibutsu] - Shimosuwa Tourism Association, accessed February 11, 2021.</ref>
"Manji no Sekibutsu" is also a registered trademark owned by the Shimosuwa Chamber of Commerce.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://www.j-platpat.inpit.go.jp/c1800/TR/JP-1975-111530/F9955187CD2A8CF63F94F7E0BFD8C0A6B6A2FE9ED1871582677DE84CB61BA02A/40/ja |title=Trademark Registration No. 1326194 |access-date=2023/7/8 |publisher=National Center for Industrial Property Information and Training}}</ref>
== Overview == [[File:Manji-no sekibutsu (stone buddha) , 万治の石仏 - panoramio (20).jpg|thumb|Inscription "Namu Amida Butsu Manji 3rd Year, 11th Month, 1st Day, Ganshu Myōyo Jokō, Shinyo Hiroharu"]]
The stone Buddha measures 2.6 meters in height, 3.8 meters in width, and 3.7 meters in depth. It is made of andesite (hornblende-pyroxene andesite), with a Buddha head approximately 65 centimeters tall placed on a massive stone body.<ref name=kanko/><ref name=":4" /> The front of the body features a carved [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] seated in {{ill|mudra|lt=Dhyanamudra|ja|印相#阿弥陀如来の印相|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mudra|qq=|1=mudra|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q208030}}.<ref name=town/><ref name=":4" />
The robe is adorned with symbols such as [[:en:Swastika|Swastika]] (right-facing), the sun, thunder, clouds, rocks, and the moon, reflecting esoteric [[:en:Mikkyō|Mikkyō]] mandalas. These elements are attributed to the "One Body, Two Buddhas" doctrine of a sect of sculpting priests from the [[:en:Jōdo-shū|Jōdo-shū]], combining [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] with [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name=town/><ref name=":4" />
== Legend == A local legend recounts that during the construction of a large [[:en:Torii|torii]] for Suwa Taisha in [[:en:1657|1657]] (Meireki 3), a stonecutter attempted to use the current stone Buddha as material. However, upon striking it with a chisel, the stone bled. Afraid of divine retribution, the construction ceased. The stone was later consecrated as an Amida Nyorai.<ref name=town/><ref name=kanko/>
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Manji-no-sekibutsu01n4592.jpg|Front view File:Manji-no-sekibutsu06n4592.jpg|Rear view File:Manji-no-sekibutsu05n4592.jpg|Face File:Manji-no-sekibutsu07s4592.jpg|Hand mudra </gallery>
== Access == * 16 minutes by bus from [[:en:Shimo-Suwa Station|Shimosuwa Station]] (Miyanoue bus stop) * [[:en:Japan National Route 20|National Route 20 (Japan)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Manji-no-sekibutsu]][[ja:万治の石仏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11353878]] Wikidata name: Manji no Sekibutsu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox artwork | image_file = Yashiro, Shimosuwa, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture 393-0000, Japan - panoramio.jpg | painting_alignment = right | image_size = 300px | title = Manji no Sekibutsu | caption = Front view | year = [[:en:1660|1660]] (Manji 3) | type = {{ill|Stone Buddha|ja|石仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Buddha|qq=}} | material = [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] (hornblende-pyroxene andesite) | height_metric = 2.6 | width_metric = 3.8 | metric_unit = m | accession = | city = [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] | museum = | website = {{URL|1=https://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000750/index.html|2=Manji no Sekibutsu (Shimosuwa Town)}} }}
'''Manji no Sekibutsu''' (万治の石仏) is a {{ill|Stone Buddha|lt=Stone Buddha|lt=Stone Buddha statue|ja|石仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone Buddha statue|qq=}} located in [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. It was created in the early Edo period in [[:en:1660|1660]] (Manji 3). Owned by Shimosuwa Town, it has been designated a Municipal Cultural Property since [[:en:1982|1982]] (Shōwa 57) under the name "Manji no Sekibutsu (Mitarashi no Sekibutsu)."<ref name=town>[http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000750/index.html Manji no Sekibutsu] - Shimosuwa Town, accessed February 11, 2021.</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1496969553133/files/5-106.pdf |title=Municipal Cultural Property No. 34 |access-date=2023/8/20 |publisher=Shimosuwa Town}}</ref> It is situated across the {{ill|Togawa River|ja|砥川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togawa River|qq=}} from the {{ill|Suwa Taisha Shimosha Spring Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#下社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha Shimosha Spring Shrine|1=Suwa taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q218813}}. The local area name "Sekibutsu" is derived from this statue.
The stone Buddha gained national recognition in the [[:en:1970s|1970s]] when it was introduced by the artist [[:en:Tarō Okamoto|Taro Okamoto]].<ref name=kanko>[https://shimosuwaonsen.jp/万治の石仏/ Manji no Sekibutsu] - Shimosuwa Tourism Association, accessed February 11, 2021.</ref>
"Manji no Sekibutsu" is also a registered trademark owned by the Shimosuwa Chamber of Commerce.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://www.j-platpat.inpit.go.jp/c1800/TR/JP-1975-111530/F9955187CD2A8CF63F94F7E0BFD8C0A6B6A2FE9ED1871582677DE84CB61BA02A/40/ja |title=Trademark Registration No. 1326194 |access-date=2023/7/8 |publisher=National Center for Industrial Property Information and Training}}</ref>
== Overview == [[File:Manji-no sekibutsu (stone buddha) , 万治の石仏 - panoramio (20).jpg|thumb|Inscription "Namu Amida Butsu Manji 3rd Year, 11th Month, 1st Day, Ganshu Myōyo Jokō, Shinyo Hiroharu"]]
The stone Buddha measures 2.6 meters in height, 3.8 meters in width, and 3.7 meters in depth. It is made of andesite (hornblende-pyroxene andesite), with a Buddha head approximately 65 centimeters tall placed on a massive stone body.<ref name=kanko/><ref name=":4" /> The front of the body features a carved [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] seated in {{ill|mudra|lt=Dhyanamudra|ja|印相#阿弥陀如来の印相|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mudra|1=mudra|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q208030}}.<ref name=town/><ref name=":4" />
The robe is adorned with symbols such as [[:en:Swastika|Swastika]] (right-facing), the sun, thunder, clouds, rocks, and the moon, reflecting esoteric [[:en:Mikkyō|Mikkyō]] mandalas. These elements are attributed to the "One Body, Two Buddhas" doctrine of a sect of sculpting priests from the [[:en:Jōdo-shū|Jōdo-shū]], combining [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] with [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name=town/><ref name=":4" />
== Legend == A local legend recounts that during the construction of a large [[:en:Torii|torii]] for Suwa Taisha in [[:en:1657|1657]] (Meireki 3), a stonecutter attempted to use the current stone Buddha as material. However, upon striking it with a chisel, the stone bled. Afraid of divine retribution, the construction ceased. The stone was later consecrated as an Amida Nyorai.<ref name=town/><ref name=kanko/>
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Manji-no-sekibutsu01n4592.jpg|Front view File:Manji-no-sekibutsu06n4592.jpg|Rear view File:Manji-no-sekibutsu05n4592.jpg|Face File:Manji-no-sekibutsu07s4592.jpg|Hand mudra </gallery>
== Access == * 16 minutes by bus from [[:en:Shimo-Suwa Station|Shimosuwa Station]] (Miyanoue bus stop) * [[:en:Japan National Route 20|National Route 20 (Japan)]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Manji-no-sekibutsu]][[ja:万治の石仏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11353878]] Wikidata name: Manji no Sekibutsu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox artwork | image_file = Yashiro, Shimosuwa, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture 393-0000, Japan - panoramio.jpg | painting_alignment = right | image_size = 300px | title = Manji no Sekibutsu | caption = Front view | year = [[:en:1660|1660]] (Manji 3) | type = {{ill|Stone Buddha|ja|石仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Buddha|qq=}} | material = [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] (hornblende-pyroxene andesite) | height_metric = 2.6 | width_metric = 3.8 | metric_unit = m | accession = | city = [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] | museum = | website = {{URL|1=https://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000750/index.html|2=Manji no Sekibutsu (Shimosuwa Town)}} }}
'''Manji no Sekibutsu''' (万治の石仏) is a {{ill|Stone Buddha|lt=Stone Buddha|lt=Stone Buddha statue|ja|石仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone Buddha statue|qq=}} located in [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. It was created in the early Edo period in [[:en:1660|1660]] (Manji 3). Owned by Shimosuwa Town, it has been designated a Municipal Cultural Property since [[:en:1982|1982]] (Shōwa 57) under the name "Manji no Sekibutsu (Mitarashi no Sekibutsu)."<ref name=town>[http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000750/index.html Manji no Sekibutsu] - Shimosuwa Town, accessed February 11, 2021.</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1496969553133/files/5-106.pdf |title=Municipal Cultural Property No. 34 |access-date=2023/8/20 |publisher=Shimosuwa Town}}</ref> It is situated across the {{ill|Togawa River|ja|砥川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togawa River|qq=}} from the {{ill|Suwa Taisha Shimosha Spring Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#下社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha Shimosha Spring Shrine|1=Suwa taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q218813}}. The local area name "Sekibutsu" is derived from this statue.
The stone Buddha gained national recognition in the [[:en:1970s|1970s]] when it was introduced by the artist [[:en:Tarō Okamoto|Taro Okamoto]].<ref name=kanko>[https://shimosuwaonsen.jp/万治の石仏/ Manji no Sekibutsu] - Shimosuwa Tourism Association, accessed February 11, 2021.</ref>
"Manji no Sekibutsu" is also a registered trademark owned by the Shimosuwa Chamber of Commerce.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://www.j-platpat.inpit.go.jp/c1800/TR/JP-1975-111530/F9955187CD2A8CF63F94F7E0BFD8C0A6B6A2FE9ED1871582677DE84CB61BA02A/40/ja |title=Trademark Registration No. 1326194 |access-date=2023/7/8 |publisher=National Center for Industrial Property Information and Training}}</ref>
== Overview == [[File:Manji-no sekibutsu (stone buddha) , 万治の石仏 - panoramio (20).jpg|thumb|Inscription "Namu Amida Butsu Manji 3rd Year, 11th Month, 1st Day, Ganshu Myōyo Jokō, Shinyo Hiroharu"]]
The stone Buddha measures 2.6 meters in height, 3.8 meters in width, and 3.7 meters in depth. It is made of andesite (hornblende-pyroxene andesite), with a Buddha head approximately 65 centimeters tall placed on a massive stone body.<ref name=kanko/><ref name=":4" /> The front of the body features a carved [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] seated in {{ill|mudra|lt=Dhyanamudra|ja|印相#阿弥陀如来の印相|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mudra|1=mudra|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q208030}}.<ref name=town/><ref name=":4" />
The robe is adorned with symbols such as [[:en:Swastika|Swastika]] (right-facing), the sun, thunder, clouds, rocks, and the moon, reflecting esoteric [[:en:Mikkyō|Mikkyō]] mandalas. These elements are attributed to the "One Body, Two Buddhas" doctrine of a sect of sculpting priests from the [[:en:Jōdo-shū|Jōdo-shū]], combining [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] with [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name=town/><ref name=":4" />
== Legend == A local legend recounts that during the construction of a large [[:en:Torii|torii]] for Suwa Taisha in [[:en:1657|1657]] (Meireki 3), a stonecutter attempted to use the current stone Buddha as material. However, upon striking it with a chisel, the stone bled. Afraid of divine retribution, the construction ceased. The stone was later consecrated as an Amida Nyorai.<ref name=town/><ref name=kanko/>
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Manji-no-sekibutsu01n4592.jpg|Front view File:Manji-no-sekibutsu06n4592.jpg|Rear view File:Manji-no-sekibutsu05n4592.jpg|Face File:Manji-no-sekibutsu07s4592.jpg|Hand mudra </gallery>
== Access == * 16 minutes by bus from [[:en:Shimo-Suwa Station|Shimosuwa Station]] (Miyanoue bus stop) * [[:en:Japan National Route 20|National Route 20 (Japan)]]
== Nearby == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha Shimosha Spring Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#下社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha Shimosha Spring Shrine|1=Suwa taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q218813}} * {{ill|Jiunji Temple (Nagano)|ja|慈雲寺 (長野県下諏訪町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jiunji Temple (Nagano)|1=Jiun-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495049}} * {{ill|Suigetsu Park|ja|水月公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suigetsu Park|1=Suigetsu Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109073780}} * Grave of {{ill|Yamatokentomotsu|ja|大和監物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatokentomotsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Yajoseki|ja|矢除石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yajoseki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== References == {{Reflist|25em}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Landmarks of Shimosuwa|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Landmarks of Shimosuwa|1=Shimosuwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1204211}} * [[:en:Tarō Okamoto|Taro Okamoto]] * [[:en:Jirō Nitta|Jiro Nitta]] * {{ill|Kazuo Ichikawa|ja|市川一雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuo Ichikawa|1=Ichikawa Kazuo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106478628}}

These elements are attributed to the "One Body, Two Buddhas" doctrine of a sect of sculpting priests from the [[:en:Jōdo-shū|Jōdo-shū]], combining [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] with [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name=town/><ref name=":4" />
== Legend == A local legend recounts that during the construction of a large [[:en:Torii|torii]] for Suwa Taisha in [[:en:1657|1657]] (Meireki 3), a stonecutter attempted to use the current stone Buddha as material. However, upon striking it with a chisel, the stone bled. Afraid of divine retribution, the construction ceased. The stone was later consecrated as an Amida Nyorai.<ref name=town/><ref name=kanko/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Manji-no-sekibutsu]][[ja:万治の石仏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11353878]] Wikidata name: Manji no Sekibutsu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox artwork | image_file = Yashiro, Shimosuwa, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture 393-0000, Japan - panoramio.jpg | painting_alignment = right | image_size = 300px | title = Manji no Sekibutsu | caption = Front view | year = [[:en:1660|1660]] (Manji 3) | type = {{ill|Stone Buddha|ja|石仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Buddha|qq=}} | material = [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] (hornblende-pyroxene andesite) | height_metric = 2.6 | width_metric = 3.8 | metric_unit = m | accession = | city = [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] | museum = | website = {{URL|1=https://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000750/index.html|2=Manji no Sekibutsu (Shimosuwa Town)}} }}
'''Manji no Sekibutsu''' (万治の石仏) is a {{ill|Stone Buddha|lt=Stone Buddha|lt=Stone Buddha statue|ja|石仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone Buddha statue|qq=}} located in [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. It was created in the early Edo period in [[:en:1660|1660]] (Manji 3). Owned by Shimosuwa Town, it has been designated a Municipal Cultural Property since [[:en:1982|1982]] (Shōwa 57) under the name "Manji no Sekibutsu (Mitarashi no Sekibutsu)."<ref name=town>[http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000750/index.html Manji no Sekibutsu] - Shimosuwa Town, accessed February 11, 2021.</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1496969553133/files/5-106.pdf |title=Municipal Cultural Property No. 34 |access-date=2023/8/20 |publisher=Shimosuwa Town}}</ref> It is situated across the {{ill|Togawa River|ja|砥川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togawa River|qq=}} from the {{ill|Suwa Taisha Shimosha Spring Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#下社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha Shimosha Spring Shrine|1=Suwa taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q218813}}. The local area name "Sekibutsu" is derived from this statue.
The stone Buddha gained national recognition in the [[:en:1970s|1970s]] when it was introduced by the artist [[:en:Tarō Okamoto|Taro Okamoto]].<ref name=kanko>[https://shimosuwaonsen.jp/万治の石仏/ Manji no Sekibutsu] - Shimosuwa Tourism Association, accessed February 11, 2021.</ref>
"Manji no Sekibutsu" is also a registered trademark owned by the Shimosuwa Chamber of Commerce.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://www.j-platpat.inpit.go.jp/c1800/TR/JP-1975-111530/F9955187CD2A8CF63F94F7E0BFD8C0A6B6A2FE9ED1871582677DE84CB61BA02A/40/ja |title=Trademark Registration No. 1326194 |access-date=2023/7/8 |publisher=National Center for Industrial Property Information and Training}}</ref>
== Overview == [[File:Manji-no sekibutsu (stone buddha) , 万治の石仏 - panoramio (20).jpg|thumb|Inscription "Namu Amida Butsu Manji 3rd Year, 11th Month, 1st Day, Ganshu Myōyo Jokō, Shinyo Hiroharu"]]
The stone Buddha measures 2.6 meters in height, 3.8 meters in width, and 3.7 meters in depth. It is made of andesite (hornblende-pyroxene andesite), with a Buddha head approximately 65 centimeters tall placed on a massive stone body.<ref name=kanko/><ref name=":4" /> The front of the body features a carved [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] seated in {{ill|mudra|lt=Dhyanamudra|ja|印相#阿弥陀如来の印相|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mudra|1=mudra|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q208030}}.<ref name=town/><ref name=":4" />
The robe is adorned with symbols such as [[:en:Swastika|Swastika]] (right-facing), the sun, thunder, clouds, rocks, and the moon, reflecting esoteric [[:en:Mikkyō|Mikkyō]] mandalas. These elements are attributed to the "One Body, Two Buddhas" doctrine of a sect of sculpting priests from the [[:en:Jōdo-shū|Jōdo-shū]], combining [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] with [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name=town/><ref name=":4" />
== Legend == A local legend recounts that during the construction of a large [[:en:Torii|torii]] for Suwa Taisha in [[:en:1657|1657]] (Meireki 3), a stonecutter attempted to use the current stone Buddha as material. However, upon striking it with a chisel, the stone bled. Afraid of divine retribution, the construction ceased. The stone was later consecrated as an Amida Nyorai.<ref name=town/><ref name=kanko/>
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Manji-no-sekibutsu01n4592.jpg|Front view File:Manji-no-sekibutsu06n4592.jpg|Rear view File:Manji-no-sekibutsu05n4592.jpg|Face File:Manji-no-sekibutsu07s4592.jpg|Hand mudra </gallery>

The local area name "Sekibutsu" is derived from this statue.
The stone Buddha gained national recognition in the [[:en:1970s|1970s]] when it was introduced by the artist [[:en:Tarō Okamoto|Taro Okamoto]].<ref name=kanko>[https://shimosuwaonsen.jp/万治の石仏/ Manji no Sekibutsu] - Shimosuwa Tourism Association, accessed February 11, 2021.</ref>
"Manji no Sekibutsu" is also a registered trademark owned by the Shimosuwa Chamber of Commerce.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://www.j-platpat.inpit.go.jp/c1800/TR/JP-1975-111530/F9955187CD2A8CF63F94F7E0BFD8C0A6B6A2FE9ED1871582677DE84CB61BA02A/40/ja |title=Trademark Registration No. 1326194 |access-date=2023/7/8 |publisher=National Center for Industrial Property Information and Training}}</ref>
== Overview == [[File:Manji-no sekibutsu (stone buddha) , 万治の石仏 - panoramio (20).jpg|thumb|Inscription "Namu Amida Butsu Manji 3rd Year, 11th Month, 1st Day, Ganshu Myōyo Jokō, Shinyo Hiroharu"]]
The stone Buddha measures 2.6 meters in height, 3.8 meters in width, and 3.7 meters in depth. It is made of andesite (hornblende-pyroxene andesite), with a Buddha head approximately 65 centimeters tall placed on a massive stone body.<ref name=kanko/><ref name=":4" /> The front of the body features a carved [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] seated in {{ill|mudra|lt=Dhyanamudra|ja|印相#阿弥陀如来の印相|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mudra|1=mudra|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q208030}}.<ref name=town/><ref name=":4" />
The robe is adorned with symbols such as [[:en:Swastika|Swastika]] (right-facing), the sun, thunder, clouds, rocks, and the moon, reflecting esoteric [[:en:Mikkyō|Mikkyō]] mandalas. These elements are attributed to the "One Body, Two Buddhas" doctrine of a sect of sculpting priests from the [[:en:Jōdo-shū|Jōdo-shū]], combining [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] with [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name=town/><ref name=":4" />
== Legend == A local legend recounts that during the construction of a large [[:en:Torii|torii]] for Suwa Taisha in [[:en:1657|1657]] (Meireki 3), a stonecutter attempted to use the current stone Buddha as material. However, upon striking it with a chisel, the stone bled. Afraid of divine retribution, the construction ceased. The stone was later consecrated as an Amida Nyorai.<ref name=town/><ref name=kanko/>
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Manji-no-sekibutsu01n4592.jpg|Front view File:Manji-no-sekibutsu06n4592.jpg|Rear view File:Manji-no-sekibutsu05n4592.jpg|Face File:Manji-no-sekibutsu07s4592.jpg|Hand mudra </gallery>
== Access == * 16 minutes by bus from [[:en:Shimo-Suwa Station|Shimosuwa Station]] (Miyanoue bus stop) * [[:en:Japan National Route 20|National Route 20 (Japan)]]
== Nearby == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha Shimosha Spring Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#下社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha Shimosha Spring Shrine|1=Suwa taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q218813}} * {{ill|Jiunji Temple (Nagano)|ja|慈雲寺 (長野県下諏訪町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jiunji Temple (Nagano)|1=Jiun-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495049}} * {{ill|Suigetsu Park|ja|水月公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suigetsu Park|1=Suigetsu Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109073780}} * Grave of {{ill|Yamatokentomotsu|ja|大和監物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatokentomotsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Yajoseki|ja|矢除石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yajoseki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== Related Articles == * {{ill|Landmarks of Shimosuwa|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Landmarks of Shimosuwa|1=Shimosuwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1204211}} * [[:en:Tarō Okamoto|Taro Okamoto]] * [[:en:Jirō Nitta|Jiro Nitta]] * {{ill|Kazuo Ichikawa|ja|市川一雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuo Ichikawa|1=Ichikawa Kazuo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106478628}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Manji-no-sekibutsu]][[ja:万治の石仏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11353878]] Wikidata name: Manji no Sekibutsu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox artwork | image_file = Yashiro, Shimosuwa, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture 393-0000, Japan - panoramio.jpg | painting_alignment = right | image_size = 300px | title = Manji no Sekibutsu | caption = Front view | year = [[:en:1660|1660]] (Manji 3) | type = {{ill|Stone Buddha|ja|石仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Buddha|qq=}} | material = [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] (hornblende-pyroxene andesite) | height_metric = 2.6 | width_metric = 3.8 | metric_unit = m | accession = | city = [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] | museum = | website = {{URL|1=https://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000750/index.html|2=Manji no Sekibutsu (Shimosuwa Town)}} }}
'''Manji no Sekibutsu''' (万治の石仏) is a {{ill|Stone Buddha|lt=Stone Buddha|lt=Stone Buddha statue|ja|石仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone Buddha statue|qq=}} located in [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. It was created in the early Edo period in [[:en:1660|1660]] (Manji 3). Owned by Shimosuwa Town, it has been designated a Municipal Cultural Property since [[:en:1982|1982]] (Shōwa 57) under the name "Manji no Sekibutsu (Mitarashi no Sekibutsu)."<ref name=town>[http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000750/index.html Manji no Sekibutsu] - Shimosuwa Town, accessed February 11, 2021.</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1496969553133/files/5-106.pdf |title=Municipal Cultural Property No. 34 |access-date=2023/8/20 |publisher=Shimosuwa Town}}</ref> It is situated across the {{ill|Togawa River|ja|砥川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togawa River|qq=}} from the {{ill|Suwa Taisha Shimosha Spring Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#下社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha Shimosha Spring Shrine|1=Suwa taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q218813}}. The local area name "Sekibutsu" is derived from this statue.
The stone Buddha gained national recognition in the [[:en:1970s|1970s]] when it was introduced by the artist [[:en:Tarō Okamoto|Taro Okamoto]].<ref name=kanko>[https://shimosuwaonsen.jp/万治の石仏/ Manji no Sekibutsu] - Shimosuwa Tourism Association, accessed February 11, 2021.</ref>
"Manji no Sekibutsu" is also a registered trademark owned by the Shimosuwa Chamber of Commerce.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://www.j-platpat.inpit.go.jp/c1800/TR/JP-1975-111530/F9955187CD2A8CF63F94F7E0BFD8C0A6B6A2FE9ED1871582677DE84CB61BA02A/40/ja |title=Trademark Registration No. 1326194 |access-date=2023/7/8 |publisher=National Center for Industrial Property Information and Training}}</ref>
== Overview == [[File:Manji-no sekibutsu (stone buddha) , 万治の石仏 - panoramio (20).jpg|thumb|Inscription "Namu Amida Butsu Manji 3rd Year, 11th Month, 1st Day, Ganshu Myōyo Jokō, Shinyo Hiroharu"]]
The stone Buddha measures 2.6 meters in height, 3.8 meters in width, and 3.7 meters in depth. It is made of andesite (hornblende-pyroxene andesite), with a Buddha head approximately 65 centimeters tall placed on a massive stone body.<ref name=kanko/><ref name=":4" /> The front of the body features a carved [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] seated in {{ill|mudra|lt=Dhyanamudra|ja|印相#阿弥陀如来の印相|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mudra|1=mudra|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q208030}}.<ref name=town/><ref name=":4" />
The robe is adorned with symbols such as [[:en:Swastika|Swastika]] (right-facing), the sun, thunder, clouds, rocks, and the moon, reflecting esoteric [[:en:Mikkyō|Mikkyō]] mandalas. These elements are attributed to the "One Body, Two Buddhas" doctrine of a sect of sculpting priests from the [[:en:Jōdo-shū|Jōdo-shū]], combining [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] with [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name=town/><ref name=":4" />
== Legend == A local legend recounts that during the construction of a large [[:en:Torii|torii]] for Suwa Taisha in [[:en:1657|1657]] (Meireki 3), a stonecutter attempted to use the current stone Buddha as material. However, upon striking it with a chisel, the stone bled. Afraid of divine retribution, the construction ceased. The stone was later consecrated as an Amida Nyorai.<ref name=town/><ref name=kanko/>

'''Manji no Sekibutsu''' (万治の石仏) is a {{ill|Stone Buddha|lt=Stone Buddha|lt=Stone Buddha statue|ja|石仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone Buddha statue|qq=}} located in [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. It was created in the early Edo period in [[:en:1660|1660]] (Manji 3). Owned by Shimosuwa Town, it has been designated a Municipal Cultural Property since [[:en:1982|1982]] (Shōwa 57) under the name "Manji no Sekibutsu (Mitarashi no Sekibutsu)."<ref name=town>[http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000750/index.html Manji no Sekibutsu] - Shimosuwa Town, accessed February 11, 2021.</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1496969553133/files/5-106.pdf |title=Municipal Cultural Property No. 34 |access-date=2023/8/20 |publisher=Shimosuwa Town}}</ref> It is situated across the {{ill|Togawa River|ja|砥川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togawa River|qq=}} from the {{ill|Suwa Taisha Shimosha Spring Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#下社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha Shimosha Spring Shrine|1=Suwa taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q218813}}. The local area name "Sekibutsu" is derived from this statue.
The stone Buddha gained national recognition in the [[:en:1970s|1970s]] when it was introduced by the artist [[:en:Tarō Okamoto|Taro Okamoto]].<ref name=kanko>[https://shimosuwaonsen.jp/万治の石仏/ Manji no Sekibutsu] - Shimosuwa Tourism Association, accessed February 11, 2021.</ref>
"Manji no Sekibutsu" is also a registered trademark owned by the Shimosuwa Chamber of Commerce.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://www.j-platpat.inpit.go.jp/c1800/TR/JP-1975-111530/F9955187CD2A8CF63F94F7E0BFD8C0A6B6A2FE9ED1871582677DE84CB61BA02A/40/ja |title=Trademark Registration No. 1326194 |access-date=2023/7/8 |publisher=National Center for Industrial Property Information and Training}}</ref>
== Overview == [[File:Manji-no sekibutsu (stone buddha) , 万治の石仏 - panoramio (20).jpg|thumb|Inscription "Namu Amida Butsu Manji 3rd Year, 11th Month, 1st Day, Ganshu Myōyo Jokō, Shinyo Hiroharu"]]
The stone Buddha measures 2.6 meters in height, 3.8 meters in width, and 3.7 meters in depth. It is made of andesite (hornblende-pyroxene andesite), with a Buddha head approximately 65 centimeters tall placed on a massive stone body.<ref name=kanko/><ref name=":4" /> The front of the body features a carved [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] seated in {{ill|mudra|lt=Dhyanamudra|ja|印相#阿弥陀如来の印相|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mudra|1=mudra|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q208030}}.<ref name=town/><ref name=":4" />
The robe is adorned with symbols such as [[:en:Swastika|Swastika]] (right-facing), the sun, thunder, clouds, rocks, and the moon, reflecting esoteric [[:en:Mikkyō|Mikkyō]] mandalas. These elements are attributed to the "One Body, Two Buddhas" doctrine of a sect of sculpting priests from the [[:en:Jōdo-shū|Jōdo-shū]], combining [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] with [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name=town/><ref name=":4" />
== Legend == A local legend recounts that during the construction of a large [[:en:Torii|torii]] for Suwa Taisha in [[:en:1657|1657]] (Meireki 3), a stonecutter attempted to use the current stone Buddha as material. However, upon striking it with a chisel, the stone bled. Afraid of divine retribution, the construction ceased. The stone was later consecrated as an Amida Nyorai.<ref name=town/><ref name=kanko/>
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Manji-no-sekibutsu01n4592.jpg|Front view File:Manji-no-sekibutsu06n4592.jpg|Rear view File:Manji-no-sekibutsu05n4592.jpg|Face File:Manji-no-sekibutsu07s4592.jpg|Hand mudra </gallery>
== Access == * 16 minutes by bus from [[:en:Shimo-Suwa Station|Shimosuwa Station]] (Miyanoue bus stop) * [[:en:Japan National Route 20|National Route 20 (Japan)]]
== Nearby == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha Shimosha Spring Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#下社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha Shimosha Spring Shrine|1=Suwa taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q218813}} * {{ill|Jiunji Temple (Nagano)|ja|慈雲寺 (長野県下諏訪町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jiunji Temple (Nagano)|1=Jiun-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495049}} * {{ill|Suigetsu Park|ja|水月公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suigetsu Park|1=Suigetsu Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109073780}} * Grave of {{ill|Yamatokentomotsu|ja|大和監物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatokentomotsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Yajoseki|ja|矢除石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yajoseki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== References == {{Reflist|25em}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Landmarks of Shimosuwa|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Landmarks of Shimosuwa|1=Shimosuwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1204211}} * [[:en:Tarō Okamoto|Taro Okamoto]] * [[:en:Jirō Nitta|Jiro Nitta]] * {{ill|Kazuo Ichikawa|ja|市川一雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuo Ichikawa|1=Ichikawa Kazuo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106478628}}

<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Manji-no-sekibutsu}} {{wikidata link|Q11353878}} [[Category:1660 establishments]] [[Category:1660 in Japan]] [[Category:Art of the Edo period]] [[Category:Buddha statues in Japan]] [[Category:Buddhism in the Edo period]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Japanese legends]] [[Category:Municipal History of Shimosuwa]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Okamoto Tarō]] [[Category:Statues of Amitābha Buddha]] [[Category:Stone Buddha statues]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Trademarks]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Manji-no-sekibutsu]][[ja:万治の石仏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Manji-no-sekibutsu]][[ja:万治の石仏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Manji-no-sekibutsu]][[ja:万治の石仏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11353878]] Wikidata name: Manji no Sekibutsu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox artwork | image_file = Yashiro, Shimosuwa, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture 393-0000, Japan - panoramio.jpg | painting_alignment = right | image_size = 300px | title = Manji no Sekibutsu | caption = Front view | year = [[:en:1660|1660]] (Manji 3) | type = {{ill|Stone Buddha|ja|石仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Buddha|qq=}} | material = [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] (hornblende-pyroxene andesite) | height_metric = 2.6 | width_metric = 3.8 | metric_unit = m | accession = | city = [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] | museum = | website = {{URL|1=https://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000750/index.html|2=Manji no Sekibutsu (Shimosuwa Town)}} }}
'''Manji no Sekibutsu''' (万治の石仏) is a {{ill|Stone Buddha|lt=Stone Buddha|lt=Stone Buddha statue|ja|石仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone Buddha statue|qq=}} located in [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. It was created in the early Edo period in [[:en:1660|1660]] (Manji 3). Owned by Shimosuwa Town, it has been designated a Municipal Cultural Property since [[:en:1982|1982]] (Shōwa 57) under the name "Manji no Sekibutsu (Mitarashi no Sekibutsu)."<ref name=town>[http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000750/index.html Manji no Sekibutsu] - Shimosuwa Town, accessed February 11, 2021.</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1496969553133/files/5-106.pdf |title=Municipal Cultural Property No. 34 |access-date=2023/8/20 |publisher=Shimosuwa Town}}</ref> It is situated across the {{ill|Togawa River|ja|砥川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togawa River|qq=}} from the {{ill|Suwa Taisha Shimosha Spring Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#下社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha Shimosha Spring Shrine|1=Suwa taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q218813}}. The local area name "Sekibutsu" is derived from this statue.
The stone Buddha gained national recognition in the [[:en:1970s|1970s]] when it was introduced by the artist [[:en:Tarō Okamoto|Taro Okamoto]].<ref name=kanko>[https://shimosuwaonsen.jp/万治の石仏/ Manji no Sekibutsu] - Shimosuwa Tourism Association, accessed February 11, 2021.</ref>
"Manji no Sekibutsu" is also a registered trademark owned by the Shimosuwa Chamber of Commerce.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://www.j-platpat.inpit.go.jp/c1800/TR/JP-1975-111530/F9955187CD2A8CF63F94F7E0BFD8C0A6B6A2FE9ED1871582677DE84CB61BA02A/40/ja |title=Trademark Registration No. 1326194 |access-date=2023/7/8 |publisher=National Center for Industrial Property Information and Training}}</ref>
== Overview == [[File:Manji-no sekibutsu (stone buddha) , 万治の石仏 - panoramio (20).jpg|thumb|Inscription "Namu Amida Butsu Manji 3rd Year, 11th Month, 1st Day, Ganshu Myōyo Jokō, Shinyo Hiroharu"]]
The stone Buddha measures 2.6 meters in height, 3.8 meters in width, and 3.7 meters in depth. It is made of andesite (hornblende-pyroxene andesite), with a Buddha head approximately 65 centimeters tall placed on a massive stone body.<ref name=kanko/><ref name=":4" /> The front of the body features a carved [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] seated in {{ill|mudra|lt=Dhyanamudra|ja|印相#阿弥陀如来の印相|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mudra|1=mudra|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q208030}}.<ref name=town/><ref name=":4" />
The robe is adorned with symbols such as [[:en:Swastika|Swastika]] (right-facing), the sun, thunder, clouds, rocks, and the moon, reflecting esoteric [[:en:Mikkyō|Mikkyō]] mandalas. These elements are attributed to the "One Body, Two Buddhas" doctrine of a sect of sculpting priests from the [[:en:Jōdo-shū|Jōdo-shū]], combining [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] with [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name=town/><ref name=":4" />
== Legend == A local legend recounts that during the construction of a large [[:en:Torii|torii]] for Suwa Taisha in [[:en:1657|1657]] (Meireki 3), a stonecutter attempted to use the current stone Buddha as material. However, upon striking it with a chisel, the stone bled. Afraid of divine retribution, the construction ceased. The stone was later consecrated as an Amida Nyorai.<ref name=town/><ref name=kanko/>
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Manji-no-sekibutsu01n4592.jpg|Front view File:Manji-no-sekibutsu06n4592.jpg|Rear view File:Manji-no-sekibutsu05n4592.jpg|Face File:Manji-no-sekibutsu07s4592.jpg|Hand mudra </gallery>
== Access == * 16 minutes by bus from [[:en:Shimo-Suwa Station|Shimosuwa Station]] (Miyanoue bus stop) * [[:en:Japan National Route 20|National Route 20 (Japan)]]

The stone Buddha measures 2.6 meters in height, 3.8 meters in width, and 3.7 meters in depth. It is made of andesite (hornblende-pyroxene andesite), with a Buddha head approximately 65 centimeters tall placed on a massive stone body.<ref name=kanko/><ref name=":4" /> The front of the body features a carved [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] seated in {{ill|mudra|lt=Dhyanamudra|ja|印相#阿弥陀如来の印相|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mudra|1=mudra|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q208030}}.<ref name=town/><ref name=":4" />
The robe is adorned with symbols such as [[:en:Swastika|Swastika]] (right-facing), the sun, thunder, clouds, rocks, and the moon, reflecting esoteric [[:en:Mikkyō|Mikkyō]] mandalas. These elements are attributed to the "One Body, Two Buddhas" doctrine of a sect of sculpting priests from the [[:en:Jōdo-shū|Jōdo-shū]], combining [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] with [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name=town/><ref name=":4" />
== Legend == A local legend recounts that during the construction of a large [[:en:Torii|torii]] for Suwa Taisha in [[:en:1657|1657]] (Meireki 3), a stonecutter attempted to use the current stone Buddha as material. However, upon striking it with a chisel, the stone bled. Afraid of divine retribution, the construction ceased. The stone was later consecrated as an Amida Nyorai.<ref name=town/><ref name=kanko/>
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Manji-no-sekibutsu01n4592.jpg|Front view File:Manji-no-sekibutsu06n4592.jpg|Rear view File:Manji-no-sekibutsu05n4592.jpg|Face File:Manji-no-sekibutsu07s4592.jpg|Hand mudra </gallery>
== Access == * 16 minutes by bus from [[:en:Shimo-Suwa Station|Shimosuwa Station]] (Miyanoue bus stop) * [[:en:Japan National Route 20|National Route 20 (Japan)]]
== Nearby == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha Shimosha Spring Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#下社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha Shimosha Spring Shrine|1=Suwa taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q218813}} * {{ill|Jiunji Temple (Nagano)|ja|慈雲寺 (長野県下諏訪町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jiunji Temple (Nagano)|1=Jiun-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495049}} * {{ill|Suigetsu Park|ja|水月公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suigetsu Park|1=Suigetsu Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109073780}} * Grave of {{ill|Yamatokentomotsu|ja|大和監物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatokentomotsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Yajoseki|ja|矢除石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yajoseki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== References == {{Reflist|25em}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Landmarks of Shimosuwa|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Landmarks of Shimosuwa|1=Shimosuwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1204211}} * [[:en:Tarō Okamoto|Taro Okamoto]] * [[:en:Jirō Nitta|Jiro Nitta]] * {{ill|Kazuo Ichikawa|ja|市川一雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuo Ichikawa|1=Ichikawa Kazuo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106478628}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Manji-no-sekibutsu]][[ja:万治の石仏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Manji-no-sekibutsu]][[ja:万治の石仏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Manji-no-sekibutsu]][[ja:万治の石仏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11353878]] Wikidata name: Manji no Sekibutsu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox artwork | image_file = Yashiro, Shimosuwa, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture 393-0000, Japan - panoramio.jpg | painting_alignment = right | image_size = 300px | title = Manji no Sekibutsu | caption = Front view | year = {{ill|1660|en|1660|WD=Q7001|lt=1660}} (Manji 3) | type = {{ill|Stone Buddha|ja|石仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Buddha|qq=}} | material = {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=Andesite}} (hornblende-pyroxene andesite) | height_metric = 2.6 | width_metric = 3.8 | metric_unit = m | accession = | city = {{ill|Shimosuwa|en|Shimosuwa|WD=Q1204211|lt=Shimosuwa, Nagano}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}} | museum = | website = {{URL|1=https://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000750/index.html|2=Manji no Sekibutsu (Shimosuwa Town)}} }}
'''Manji no Sekibutsu''' (万治の石仏) is a {{ill|Stone Buddha|lt=Stone Buddha|lt=Stone Buddha statue|ja|石仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone Buddha statue|qq=}} located in {{ill|Shimosuwa|en|Shimosuwa|WD=Q1204211|lt=Shimosuwa}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}, Japan. It was created in the early Edo period in {{ill|1660|en|1660|WD=Q7001|lt=1660}} (Manji 3). Owned by Shimosuwa Town, it has been designated a Municipal Cultural Property since {{ill|1982|en|1982|WD=Q2436|lt=1982}} (Shōwa 57) under the name "Manji no Sekibutsu (Mitarashi no Sekibutsu)."<ref name=town>[http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000750/index.html Manji no Sekibutsu] - Shimosuwa Town, accessed February 11, 2021.</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1496969553133/files/5-106.pdf |title=Municipal Cultural Property No. 34 |access-date=2023/8/20 |publisher=Shimosuwa Town}}</ref> It is situated across the {{ill|Togawa River|ja|砥川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togawa River|qq=}} from the {{ill|Suwa Taisha Shimosha Spring Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#下社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha Shimosha Spring Shrine|1=Suwa taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q218813}}. The local area name "Sekibutsu" is derived from this statue.
The stone Buddha gained national recognition in the {{ill|1970s|en|1970s|WD=Q35014|lt=1970s}} when it was introduced by the artist {{ill|Tarō Okamoto|en|Tarō Okamoto|WD=Q983942|lt=Taro Okamoto}}.<ref name=kanko>[https://shimosuwaonsen.jp/万治の石仏/ Manji no Sekibutsu] - Shimosuwa Tourism Association, accessed February 11, 2021.</ref>
"Manji no Sekibutsu" is also a registered trademark owned by the Shimosuwa Chamber of Commerce.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://www.j-platpat.inpit.go.jp/c1800/TR/JP-1975-111530/F9955187CD2A8CF63F94F7E0BFD8C0A6B6A2FE9ED1871582677DE84CB61BA02A/40/ja |title=Trademark Registration No. 1326194 |access-date=2023/7/8 |publisher=National Center for Industrial Property Information and Training}}</ref>
== Overview == [[File:Manji-no sekibutsu (stone buddha) , 万治の石仏 - panoramio (20).jpg|thumb|Inscription "Namu Amida Butsu Manji 3rd Year, 11th Month, 1st Day, Ganshu Myōyo Jokō, Shinyo Hiroharu"]]
The stone Buddha measures 2.6 meters in height, 3.8 meters in width, and 3.7 meters in depth. It is made of andesite (hornblende-pyroxene andesite), with a Buddha head approximately 65 centimeters tall placed on a massive stone body.<ref name=kanko/><ref name=":4" /> The front of the body features a carved {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|WD=Q236242|lt=Amida Nyorai}} seated in {{ill|mudra|lt=Dhyanamudra|ja|印相#阿弥陀如来の印相|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mudra|1=mudra|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q208030}}.<ref name=town/><ref name=":4" />
The robe is adorned with symbols such as {{ill|Swastika|en|Swastika|WD=Q45513|lt=Swastika}} (right-facing), the sun, thunder, clouds, rocks, and the moon, reflecting esoteric {{ill|Mikkyō|en|Mikkyō|WD=Q336102|lt=Mikkyō}} mandalas. These elements are attributed to the "One Body, Two Buddhas" doctrine of a sect of sculpting priests from the {{ill|Jōdo-shū|en|Jōdo-shū|WD=Q912208|lt=Jōdo-shū}}, combining {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|WD=Q236242|lt=Amida Nyorai}} with {{ill|Vairocana|en|Vairocana|WD=Q239847|lt=Dainichi Nyorai}}.<ref name=town/><ref name=":4" />
== Legend == A local legend recounts that during the construction of a large {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=torii}} for Suwa Taisha in {{ill|1657|en|1657|WD=Q6987|lt=1657}} (Meireki 3), a stonecutter attempted to use the current stone Buddha as material. However, upon striking it with a chisel, the stone bled. Afraid of divine retribution, the construction ceased. The stone was later consecrated as an Amida Nyorai.<ref name=town/><ref name=kanko/>
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Manji-no-sekibutsu01n4592.jpg|Front view File:Manji-no-sekibutsu06n4592.jpg|Rear view File:Manji-no-sekibutsu05n4592.jpg|Face File:Manji-no-sekibutsu07s4592.jpg|Hand mudra </gallery>
== Access == * 16 minutes by bus from {{ill|Shimo-Suwa Station|en|Shimo-Suwa Station|WD=Q7496937|lt=Shimosuwa Station}} (Miyanoue bus stop) * {{ill|Japan National Route 20|en|Japan National Route 20|WD=Q430608|lt=National Route 20 (Japan)}}
== Nearby == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha Shimosha Spring Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#下社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha Shimosha Spring Shrine|1=Suwa taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q218813}} * {{ill|Jiunji Temple (Nagano)|ja|慈雲寺 (長野県下諏訪町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jiunji Temple (Nagano)|1=Jiun-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495049}} * {{ill|Suigetsu Park|ja|水月公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suigetsu Park|1=Suigetsu Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109073780}} * Grave of {{ill|Yamatokentomotsu|ja|大和監物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatokentomotsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Yajoseki|ja|矢除石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yajoseki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== References == {{Reflist|25em}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Landmarks of Shimosuwa|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Landmarks of Shimosuwa|1=Shimosuwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1204211}} * {{ill|Tarō Okamoto|en|Tarō Okamoto|WD=Q983942|lt=Taro Okamoto}} * {{ill|Jirō Nitta|en|Jirō Nitta|WD=Q3179274|lt=Jiro Nitta}} * {{ill|Kazuo Ichikawa|ja|市川一雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuo Ichikawa|1=Ichikawa Kazuo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106478628}}
